{{Infobox location
| name = Helgard
| introduced = House of Blades
}}

''See also: [[Helgard Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Helgard is a territory that functions as a location or world, often referred to as the [[Tower of Winter]]. It is characterized by extreme cold and features environments such as endless, glowing fields of ice that possess their own horizons. Within its structure, there are at least thirty-two discovered floors; one such level is a sparsely populated area of radiant ice, while others include quarters constructed from undecorated metal or rough stone that contain patches of snow. [[Travelers]] associated with Helgard can use [[Gates]] to move through the territory as a fast route to other locations. The name is also shared by several distinct entities: a Grandmaster who is described as a large, bearded man and a highly skilled [[Traveler]]; an [[Incarnation]] that possesses icy eyes and the ability to preserve information; and a blue-skinned, horned woman who is accompanied by a private flurry of snow. Additionally, Helgard refers to a specific person or title within the narrative context.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Helgard]] is a place that can be traveled through via a Gate to reach a [[Naraka]] waypoint.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4">''House of Blades'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Helgard]] is a [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9">''House of Blades'', chapter 9</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Known as the [[Tower of Winter]], Helgard is a location consisting of thirty-two discovered floors. During a period of war within its walls, the site is overseen by a Grandmaster. The tower's history is immortalized in a stained-glass window that portrays an ancient [[Overlord]] signing a peace treaty with the native inhabitants. In more recent events, [[Valin]] successfully stole the [[frozen horn]] from the tower, and a giant spotted on the plateau is believed to have originated from Helgard.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Helgard]] is a Grandmaster.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|There is a war in [[Helgard]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Helgard]] is known as the [[Tower of Winter]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Helgard]] has thirty-two discovered floors.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] stole the [[frozen horn]] from [[Helgard]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A giant seen on the plateau was probably from [[Helgard]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Helgard]] is a location mentioned in a stained-glass window depicting an ancient [[Overlord]] signing a peace treaty with its natives.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Helgard is an [[Incarnation]] associated with certain [[Travelers]] and a [[Territory]] used for collecting and storing information. This entity, characterized by blue skin, white fur, and curling horns, resides within a multi-floored [[Tower of Winter]] and possesses the ability to summon snow. Though she was observed at the city gates months ago, [[King]] [[Zakareth]] has kept her in reserve until recently. Displaying both curiosity and resentment, Helgard can freeze objects with a blue hand and has used black ice to form jagged icicles. During her pursuit of [[Leah]], she was seen standing in a small snow flurry while targeting [[Andra]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard]] is associated with certain [[Travelers]] and an [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3">''City of Light'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard]] contains a [[Tower of Winter]] with dozens of floors.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard]] is a [[Territory]] that collects and stores information.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c4">''City of Light'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard]] contains formidable guardians.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7">''City of Light'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard]] was seen at the city gates months ago.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9">''City of Light'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard]] can summon snow.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard]] has curling horns behind her ears and stands in a small snow flurry.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c15">''City of Light'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard]] can freeze objects with a blue hand.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17">''City of Light'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard]] is an [[Incarnation]] who shows curiosity and resentment.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] has kept [[Helgard]] with him in reserve.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard]] is a horned woman with blue skin and white fur.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26">''City of Light'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard]] used black ice to create a jagged icicle pointed at [[Andra]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard]] is an [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard]] was coming after [[Leah]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“Destroy Damasca,” Grandmaster Helgard declared.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“He is personally overseeing the war in Helgard, Highness.”<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate locations]]
[[Category:Nations and empires]]
